extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo attacked one-third up the final climb Col du Portet, dropped everyone including MARTIN who chased solo, while THOMAS increased GC lead taking 3rd.
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA attacked bottom of final climb after 45min all-out bridging effort, proving early mechanical didn't compromise high-altitude performance.
VALVERDE positioned in early break specifically to service QUINTANA after bridge, revealing Movistar pre-planned long-range attack despite mechanical.
THOMAS took 3rd place time bonus while FROOME cracked 1:35 back, confirming Sky fully committed to Thomas GC over defending champion.
MARTIN attacked immediately off yellow jersey group at base, went group-by-group never to reconnect, unconventional solo chase tactic.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Dominated steep 14-15% gradients above 7000ft after 45min bridging effort post-mechanical, confirming altitude/steepness > sustained power for him
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Controlled final climb for 3rd plus bonus while co-leader Froome cracked, psychological shift complete as Sky fully backs him over Froome
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost 1:35 on final climb struggling visibly, now 2:31 down and likely to be passed by Roglic/Quintana before Paris
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Solo chased Quintana after attacking yellow group at climb base, bridged multiple groups but couldn't match Quintana's altitude performance
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: At 38 years positioned in early break for domestique work when Quintana bridged, tactical maturity enabling teammate's long-range move
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: At 21 rode strong in mountains supporting Thomas, commentary suggests Sky identifying him as future GC leader succession plan
